pattern_summary: AI coaching at the PLAYBOOK or SETUP level — analysing all trades belonging to a named strategy (e.g. 'London BOS+FVG long') together, surfacing aggregate edge, decay, regime-fit, rule-adherence delta, and time-of-day/session breakdowns. This is THE underdeveloped layer in the 2026 trading-journal market: per-trade AI is commoditised, portfolio-AI is generic, but coaching that says 'your London FVG-long has 62% WR but your NY version of the same setup is 38% — these are different plays, split them' is rare and high-leverage. The gap exists because most journals (a) tag trades only loosely (free-text 'setup' field) instead of structured playbook attachment, (b) lack a 'playbook entity' with explicit rules to score adherence against, (c) lack the cross-trade aggregation prompts that turn a setup into a system-under-evaluation.

ai_features.feature_name: Closest live examples: JournalPlus 'Playbook & rule tracker', Tradezella 'Playbook templates' (no AI yet), TraderSync per-strategy expectancy view (statistical, not narrative-AI), Tradingtick 'Playbook Builder' (pre-AI). No vendor has shipped a 'Playbook Coach' as a named first-class AI-feature in 2026.
ai_features.ai_scope: Per-playbook / per-setup — aggregated trades sharing one named strategy.
ai_features.ai_proactive_vs_reactive: Best fit PROACTIVE / scheduled (weekly playbook-stats push; cross-setup leaderboard) with a REACTIVE 'Coach this playbook' chat-button for deep dives. Per-trade pop-ups would over-trigger.
ai_features.ai_minimum_trade_threshold: Setup-level analysis needs ~15-20 trades per playbook for any directional claims, 30+ for SQN/expectancy (Tharp standard). Cold-start handled by 'directional read only, not statistical' caveats below 15.
ai_features.ai_underlying_tech: LLM (Claude or GPT-4-class) over a structured aggregation layer — SQL/JS that pre-computes WR / R-mean / R-stdev / session-breakdown / regime-breakdown per playbook, then feeds the table to LLM for narrative + recommendation.
ai_features.output_format: Structured-card per playbook (WR, expectancy, sample-size badge, regime-WR breakdown, next-action chip) + free-form narrative summary + chat for follow-up. Possibly a 'playbook health' green/amber/red badge.
ai_features.notable_prompts: (1) 'Given the table {playbook_stats_per_session_per_regime}, summarise the strongest sub-condition for {playbook}. Recommend: split into two playbooks, refine entry rule, retire, or scale.' (2) 'For each playbook with N>=15, identify whether rule-adherence correlates with R. If rule-followed-R > rule-broken-R by >0.3 then strengthen rule-enforcement; if equal, consider dropping the rule.' (3) 'Detect playbook DECAY: compare last 10 trades to prior 20 — is expectancy degrading?'

playbook_depth.playbook_concept_maturity: Frontier pattern. Most journals stop at 'tag-only'. RizeTrade and JournalPlus reach 'structured-rules + adherence-scored'. None of the AI-coach players yet ship 'AI-coached at playbook-LEVEL' as a primary feature.
playbook_depth.setup_level_ai: The empty quadrant. JournalPlus comes closest with rule-adherence correlation insights ('compliance drops to 45% after starting with a loss') — but that's behavior-level, not setup-level. TraderSync's per-strategy expectancy is statistical-only (no narrative coach). Killer feature gap: per-playbook AI that combines stats + narrative + actionable refinement.
playbook_depth.rule_adherence_scoring: Required substrate — without it the AI has no signal to coach on. Implementation: each playbook is a structured object {name, conditions: [check1, check2, ...], targets: [...], invalidation: ...}; each trade records which conditions were satisfied; adherence-score = met/total.
playbook_depth.pre_trade_validation: Natural extension — once a playbook has structured conditions, the same conditions can run as a pre-trade checklist. See pattern_pre_trade_validation.

coaching_depth.coaching_depth_level: deep_pattern_analysis — this is exactly the layer where causal claims become defensible because n>>1. A statement like 'your BOS+FVG fails 71% of the time when funding is positive >0.02% at entry' is real signal, not platitude.

concrete_implementations: JournalPlus: playbook attachment + adherence-correlation insights (behavior-level, $159 one-time). Strongest substrate, weakest AI narrative layer.
TraderSync: per-strategy WR/expectancy charts + Cypher AI chat (chat can be steered to playbook-level but doesn't proactively coach it).
Tradezella: Playbook templates + Zella Score (no AI narrative at playbook level, only at portfolio level).
Tradingtick: Playbook Builder + free-forever model + pre-AI roadmap; foundation present, AI not yet shipped.
RizeTrade: playbook-as-checklist + rule-follow scoring per trade; closest to a true playbook entity, no AI.
critique: Vendors have stalled in this quadrant because the SUBSTRATE (structured playbooks with explicit rules) is hard work that users resist — most traders don't formalise their playbooks beyond a free-text tag. Without that substrate AI has nothing to aggregate. The break-through product will INVERT this: ship a Bellafiore-PlayBook-style 5-variable structured template that's so easy to fill in (1 click on a closed trade: 'Save as play') that the substrate builds itself, then ship the aggregation+narrative AI on top.
opportunity_gap_crypto_perp: Crypto-perps are an UNFAIR ADVANTAGE quadrant for setup-level AI: (a) 24/7 markets give clear session-cuts (Asia/London/NY) where the same playbook performs differently — perfect for 'split this playbook' coaching, (b) funding-rate / OI / liquidation-clusters are structured per-bar data that can be auto-correlated with each playbook's outcomes (no equivalent in stocks), (c) partial-close culture means each playbook has TP1/TP2/TP3 hit-rates that vary by regime — rich aggregation opportunity. A 'BOS+FVG: 62% WR London / 38% NY / TP3-hit-rate degrades when funding > 0.02%' card per playbook would be category-defining.
uncertain: Whether JournalPlus's pattern-detection counts as 'AI-coached at playbook-level' or stays at behavior-level (need product walkthrough to verify)
Whether any 2026 stealth-mode startup has shipped exactly this (probability moderate; check Hacker News / Product Hunt monthly)
